title: 5-HTP
description: 5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP) is a naturally occurring amino acid and chemical precursor to serotonin. It is commonly used to support mood, sleep quality, and appetite regulation by increasing serotonin levels in the brain.

research.title: 5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP): Natural Occurrence, Analysis, Biosynthesis, Biotechnology, Physiology and Toxicology
research.summary: Comprehensive review covering 5-HTP's role in neurological and metabolic health, discussing its production and uses in treating depression and anxiety.

research.title: Tryptophan-enriched diet or 5-hydroxytryptophan supplementation given in a randomized controlled trial impacts social cognition
research.summary: Study found 5-HTP influences social cognition, particularly in interpreting moral and emotional cues, with increased empathy-related brain activity.

research.title: The impact of 5-hydroxytryptophan supplementation on sleep quality and gut microbiota composition in older adults
research.summary: Twelve-week trial demonstrated 5-HTP improved sleep quality and increased microbiota diversity in older adults.

research.title: Advances in the Microbial Synthesis of 5-Hydroxytryptophan
research.summary: Review discussing recent advancements in microbial methods for 5-HTP production, improving biosynthesis efficiency.

research.title: Simultaneous determination of 5-hydroxytryptophan and 3-O-methyldopa in dried blood spot by UPLC-MS/MS
research.summary: Study developed a diagnostic method to measure 5-HTP, aiding detection of L-amino acid decarboxylase deficiency.

research.title: Amines and the rat exocrine pancreas: Effects of receptor blockers on turnover of L-5HTP
research.summary: Research studied 5-HTP metabolism in the pancreas, showing changes in serotonin accumulation with receptor blockers.

research.title: Analgesic effects of N-acetyl-5HTP-5HTP amide are not directly related to brain serotonin levels
research.summary: Synthetic 5-HTP dipeptide increased pain thresholds in rats through an alternate pathway independent of serotonin.

research.title: HVA and 5HIAA CSF measurements and 5HTP trials in some patients with involuntary movements
research.summary: 5-HTP trials showed selective therapeutic benefit in specific myoclonus conditions while worsening symptoms in other movement types.

research.title: Effect of L-5HTP on Tremorine- or Oxotremorine-Induced Tremor in Mice and Rats
research.summary: Study examined 5-HTP's potential for managing tremors and muscle rigidity, relevant to Parkinson's research.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report positive effects on mood and sleep, though some experience digestive side effects initially.

effects.label: Variable Response:
effects.description: Effectiveness varies significantly among individuals, with some experiencing dramatic improvements and others noticing minimal change.

effects.label: Works Best With Cofactors:
effects.description: Users report better results when combining with B6 and other cofactors that support serotonin synthesis.

dosage.label: Typical Range:
dosage.description: 50-300mg daily, often taken in divided doses.

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Usually taken with meals or before bed depending on the desired effect.

dosage.label: Start Low:
dosage.description: New users often start at 50mg and gradually increase to assess tolerance.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Issues:
sideEffects.description: Nausea and digestive discomfort are common initially but often subside.

sideEffects.label: Serotonin Syndrome Risk:
sideEffects.description: Should not be combined with SSRIs or other serotonergic medications.

sideEffects.label: Drowsiness:
sideEffects.description: May cause drowsiness, particularly at higher doses.

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Found in most supplement stores and online retailers.

availability.label: Affordable:
availability.description: Generally inexpensive compared to many nootropics.
